title: Warhammer 40,000: Darktide

notes:
When Fatshark refuses to help us… we stand together and help ourselves. I tried the following and at least got to the title screen with the logo (not the menu or anything) and when trying to proceed (press [Space] to continue) I got the backend error that is common even among [[[windows users]]]. I’m on Fedora 36 right now. 1) set environment variables: ```[joni@linuxjoni02 ~]$ env | grep -i steam STEAM_COMPAT_CLIENT_INSTALL_PATH=/home/joni/.steam/steam STEAM_FRAME_FORCE_CLOSE=1 STEAM_COMPAT_DATA_PATH=/home/joni/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/compatdata ``` 2) start the game directly with proton ( I have Proton-GE installed): ```[joni@linuxjoni02 ~]$ ~/.local/share/Steam/compatibilitytools.d/GE-Proton7-37/proton run ~/opt_nvme/misc/SteamLibrary/steamapps/common/Warhammer\ 40,000\ Darktide\ Playtest/binaries/Darktide.exe fsync: up and running. wine: RLIMIT_NICE is <= 20, unable to use setpriority safely ```
=> the game should boot up to the title screen I hope the launcher isn't necassary to "authenticate" us or something stupid.
title: Warhammer 40,000: Darktide

title: Warhammer 40,000: Darktide

notes:
I've tried Proton Experimental and the most recent Glorious Eggroll and Proton builds. My friends on windows are having issues with the game as well. However in their case it's just a much longer wait on the same 'shader cache' window compared to just hanging on Linux. 

I'm assuming this is just a temporary bug, and I'll update my report when/if the game is playable again in the future.

notes:
The game worked until the latest patch 1.0.20 without any major problems. You had some random crashes and the game needed some attempts to start without crashing but as soon as you managed to get to the start screen it worked. I'm no longer able to get pass the shader build. Sometimes even the Launcher itself hangs. 

I've played this with people using Windows and they had more crashes than me.
